title: FOOSH Injury - Colles Fracture
chief_complaint: 65-year-old female slipped on ice and fell on outstretched right hand (FOOSH). Immediate wrist pain, swelling, and deformity. Unable to move wrist.

physical_exam.wrist: Dinner fork deformity, swelling, tenderness over distal radius, intact median nerve function, radial pulse present

hidden_data.xray_wrist: Extra-articular distal radius fracture with dorsal angulation and displacement, ulnar styloid fracture

correct_diagnosis: Colles Fracture (Distal Radius Fracture with Dorsal Angulation)
key_learning_points: Check median nerve function before and after reduction

patient_responses.fall: I slipped on ice and put my hand out to break the fall

feedback.incorrect_message: This is a Colles fracture. The FOOSH + dinner fork deformity confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Colles fracture: Closed reduction under hematoma block or sedation. Below-elbow cast for 6 weeks. Repeat X-ray at 1 and 2 weeks to check alignment. Surgery (ORIF with volar plate) if intra-articular, unstable, or loss of reduction.
